title: Does Seagate (STX) Using Debt-for-Equity Swaps Signal a New AI-Era Capital Strategy?
description:
Earlier in May, Seagate Technology Holdings and its subsidiary Seagate HDD Cayman privately exchanged US$185.91 million of 3.50% Exchangeable Senior Notes due 2028 for a mix of cash and ordinary shares, retiring those securities while leaving roughly US$185.80 million of the notes outstanding on unchanged terms.
This balance-sheet move, alongside insider share sales and strong enthusiasm around AI-driven storage demand, highlights how capital structure and sentiment are evolving as investors...
